content:
• The unique icon indicates a card that represents a singular person, place, or thing within the Marvel universe. Two unique cards are considered to 'match' if they share a title with no subtitle/alter-ego title on either, or if the subtitle/alter-ego title of one matches the title/subtitle/alter-ego title of the other.
  • During deckbuilding, a player cannot include multiple matching cards in their deck (the identity is included in this evaluation); once setup has begun, a player is not prevented from adding matching cards via game effects.
  • When choosing identities during setup, players cannot choose matching identities (though the scenario may still be chosen even if a villain matches a chosen identity).
  • A non-villain card in an out-of-play state matching a card in play cannot enter play — a player card cannot be played/put into play; a non-villain encounter card is discarded (or, if being revealed, the player is dealt a facedown encounter card instead).

Upgrade is a player card type representing powers, attacks, equipment, and other assets at an identity's immediate disposal.

  • An upgrade is active so long as it's in play, remaining until a card ability causes it to leave. Most upgrade cards enter play near a player's identity card, modifying the hero or alter-ego (or both).
  • Some upgrades 'attach to' another card, modifying that card instead.
  • A player controls any upgrades attached to characters they control, including upgrades owned by another player.
  • Unless attached to a different friendly character, upgrade cards are considered an extension of the controlling player's identity.

• When a card with this keyword enters play, place X all-purpose counters from the token pool on the card; the word following the value identifies the type of use.
  • Each card with this keyword also has an ability referencing that use type as part of its cost.
  • When the last all-purpose counter is removed, discard the card. Equivalent to: 'This card enters play with X all-purpose counters. These are 'type counters.'
  • If there are no all-purpose counters on this card, discard this card.'
